subheadline: we're the makers of devin — The AI software engineer
value_proposition: Cognition builds AI agents starting with Devin, an AI software engineer that can autonomously handle software development tasks.
target_audience: Software engineering teams, CTOs, and enterprises looking to automate or augment software development with AI agents.

pricing.price_anchoring: Pricing page exists in nav; blog post 'New self-serve plans for Devin' signals recent democratization of access, implying enterprise was the prior entry point

features: AI software engineer (Devin) for autonomous coding tasks
Cloud agent handoff from local terminal (Devin for Terminal)
COBOL and legacy modernization for enterprises
differentiators: Self-described as an 'Agent Lab' — positioning beyond a single product
Founding team holds 10 IOI gold medals
Team pedigree: Cursor, Scale AI, Google DeepMind, Waymo, Nuro, Lunchclub, Modal
Global enterprise partnerships (Mercedes-Benz, Fortune 500, Japan, Singapore APAC expansion)
trust_signals: Japan market launch with named partner
~53 open roles across 5 continents signals institutional scale

design_patterns.layout: Minimal hero-first single-column layout with a rotating/bracketed product callout, followed by a blog feed and careers section
design_patterns.color_scheme: Likely dark background with high-contrast white/light text based on bold minimal aesthetic
design_patterns.typography: Clean, modern sans-serif; uses lowercase for brand voice ('cognition is an Agent Lab'); bracket notation for labels
notable_patterns: Bracket/array notation for product callouts (e.g., '[ devin / The AI software engineer ]')
Rotating or animated product tagline in hero
Blog feed as social proof/momentum signal on homepage

emotional_tone: Confident and aspirational with a technical, understated cool — projects inevitability rather than hype
key_objections_addressed: Is the team credible? — Addressed with IOI gold medals and named pedigree companies
Is this enterprise-ready? — Addressed with Fortune 500, Mercedes-Benz, and government offerings
Is it globally supported? — Addressed with Singapore, Japan, UK, India, ANZ, MENA presence
Is it only for large companies? — Partially addressed with 'New self-serve plans' blog post
missing_or_weak: No customer logos or testimonials visible on the homepage
No pricing details or even price ranges shown without clicking away
No product screenshots, demo video, or visual proof of Devin in action
No explicit ROI metrics or case study statistics (e.g., 'saved X hours')
Value of Windsurf product is completely unexplained on the homepage
No live demo or interactive CTA beyond 'Try devin'
Blog used as social proof but lacks pull quotes or highlighted outcomes
standout_elements: 'Agent Lab' positioning is bold and category-defining rather than feature-listing
Team credentials section is unusually strong and specific (IOI medals, named companies)
Bracket/array syntax creates a distinctive, memorable visual language tied to developer identity
Blog feed on homepage demonstrates consistent momentum and thought leadership
Careers section doubles as a trust signal — 53 open roles signals rapid, well-funded growth
Government product line signals security, trust, and institutional legitimacy without over-explaining
